data.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I can install artifacts with a version stamp so that host projects can detect staleness
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Embed a version stamp in each artifact that `dso-setup.sh` installs in host projects. Text artifacts use `# dso-version: <version>` as a comment; YAML artifacts use `x-dso-version: <version>` as a top-level YAML key (not a comment, to survive PyYAML round-trip serialization). Version is read from `plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`.

**Why**: Without a version stamp, there is no way for host projects to detect staleness. The stamp is the foundational mechanism that enables both the session-start notification (S2) and the explicit update command (S3) to work correctly.

**Scope**:
- IN: Version stamp in all 4 artifacts: shim (`.claude/scripts/dso`), `dso-config.conf`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- IN: `.claude/dso-artifact-check-cache` added to host project `.gitignore` during `dso-setup.sh` install (required by S2 cache mechanism)
- IN: `# dso-version:` designated as reserved metadata in `dso-config.conf`, distinct from user-intentional commented-out config keys — merge logic in S3 must never treat it as a user exclusion
- IN: YAML artifacts use `x-dso-version: <version>` (top-level YAML key) to survive `merge_precommit_hooks` and `merge_ci_workflow` round-trips
- IN: Tests updated to verify stamp presence in all 4 artifacts, including a round-trip verification
- OUT: Logic that reads/compares stamps — that is S2's scope
- OUT: Logic that updates stamps during artifact sync — that is S3's scope

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `dso-setup.sh` embeds `# dso-version: <version>` in the first 5 lines of the shim (`.claude/scripts/dso`) and `dso-config.conf`, and embeds `x-dso-version: <version>` as a top-level YAML key in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, reading the version from `plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`
  ← Satisfies: "Version stamps enable host projects to detect stale artifacts"
- When this story is complete, the stamp is preserved (idempotent) when `dso-setup.sh` is re-run on an already-stamped artifact
  ← Satisfies: "Version stamps enable host projects to detect stale artifacts"
- When this story is complete, `# dso-version:` is designated as reserved metadata in `dso-config.conf`; the key-additive merge logic in S3 must treat the stamp line distinctly from commented-out config keys and never suppress or skip updating it
  ← Satisfies: "Config merge preserves user customizations without corrupting metadata"
- When this story is complete, `dso-setup.sh` appends `.claude/dso-artifact-check-cache` to the host project's `.gitignore` during install (if not already present)
  ← Satisfies: "Cache file does not pollute host project git status"
- When this story is complete, `tests/scripts/test-dso-setup.sh` verifies the stamp is present in all 4 artifacts after a fresh install, including a round-trip test that confirms the YAML stamp (`x-dso-version`) survives `merge_ci_workflow` and `merge_precommit_hooks` processing
  ← Satisfies: "Version stamps survive the update mechanism without creating an infinite staleness loop"
- When this story is complete, `tests/scripts/test-marketplace-json.sh` asserts that the stamp format is consistent across all 4 artifacts
  ← Satisfies: "Version stamps enable host projects to detect stale artifacts"
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh` does not false-positive on `# dso-version:` or `x-dso-version:` in artifact files
  ← Satisfies: "Stamp mechanism does not break existing validation"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] YAML artifacts use `x-dso-version` as a top-level YAML key — PyYAML strips `#` comments on round-trip serialization, which would create an infinite staleness loop if a comment-based stamp were used
- [Reliability] `# dso-version:` in `dso-config.conf` must be reserved metadata; the key-additive merge in S3 must distinguish it from `# SOME_KEY=value` commented-out config keys

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
